keyPoints: Two-arm comparison of public-interest AI versus private AI to assess impact on collaboration quality and output diversity.

Treat governance dimensions—public justification, equality/human rights safeguards, deliberation/co-design, technical safeguards, and openness to validation—as central mediators/moderators of outcomes.
Employ a robust mixed-methods design (quantitative plus qualitative) with validated HAIC and novelty measures to capture process and product effects.
Prioritize rigorous experimental designs (randomized or stepped-wedge) with pre-registration and mediation/moderation analyses.
Center governance artifacts (deliberation depth, transparency, auditability, public-interest framing) as primary data sources.
Acknowledge limitations and design for cross-domain replication to assess generalizability beyond a single sector.
Translate findings into practical design heuristics for engineers and policy recommendations for public-interest AI deployment.
